routing.task_class: Explicit Coding Agents workflow-state and source CLI continuation, audit, or repair
routing.primary_route: Use Coding Agents only after an explicit trigger, preserve .coding-agents workflow records, and dispatch actual workers only through official Codex subagent spawn tools.
fallback_conditions: When official Codex subagent tools are unavailable, keep execution in the parent thread and do not launch a CLI process runner.
non_use_conditions: Generic coding, debugging, source changes, delegation, or subagent coordination do not trigger Coding Agents.

inputs.name: Explicit workflow request
inputs.description: A user-named Coding Agents request or a request to continue, audit, or repair existing .coding-agents state or the source CLI.

inputs.name: Target repository and workflow identity
inputs.description: The target Git worktree plus task_id, epoch, scope, and optional work type or assignment overlay.

outputs.name: Workflow-state packets
outputs.description: Validated .coding-agents intake, compact shared-contract scaffold, assignment, collection, finalization, orchestration-skeleton, audit, and handoff records.

outputs.name: Official subagent dispatch boundary
outputs.description: Actual workers are dispatched only through official Codex subagent spawn tools; the CLI emits no Codex child process.

rules.description: The source CLI records assignments and orchestration skeletons but never launches Codex workers; actual dispatch uses only official Codex subagent spawn tools.

The .coding-agents intake keeps shared scaffold contracts compact and task coverage outcome-focused while assignment, collection, finalization, handoff, doctor, verification, lifecycle, full dispatched-packet contracts, and historical packet compatibility remain enforced.
